title: allium neapolitanum
intro: Allium neapolitanum, also known as the Naples garlic or winter alyssum, is a perennial plant in the garlic family (Allium). It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The health benefits of Allium neapolitanum include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antiseptic to protect against the growth of bacteria and fungi. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as an ingredient in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be dried and used as a culinary spice to add flavor to food.
